title: "podman pull" ignores storage.conf
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Due to `$HOME` being in NFS, I have a `storage.conf` which relocates the graphroot.  `podman pull` seems to ignore settings there and tries to access `$HOME/.local/share.containers`.

This worked before (I am not sure when it broke).

My `~/.config/storage/containers.conf` is

```
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  runroot = "/run/user/505"
  graphroot = "/.local/home/ensc/.local/share/containers/storage"
```

and I get (as non-root):

```
$ podman pull   fedora:33
  open /home/ensc/.local/share/containers/sigstore/fedora@sha256=d0a84038959b1be3021cb7ffb6dae386d13a27c39519a2185eab5c4069e71295/signature-1: permission denied
```

--> it accesses `$HOME` but not the configured graphroot.  

Adding `--loglevel=debug` shows the the configured graphroot is used at some places (but ignored later):

```
$ podman pull --loglevel=debug fedora:33
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /.local/home/ensc/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
   --> OK
DEBU[0005] Reading /home/ensc/.local/share/containers/sigstore/fedora@sha256=d0a84038959b1be3021cb7ffb6dae386d13a27c39519a2185eab5c4069e71295/signature-1 
   --> BAD
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. reconfigure `graphroot` in `$HOME/.config/container/storage.conf`

2. render default graphroot inaccessible; e.g.  `chmod 000 ~/.local/share/containers`

3. try to pull an image


**Describe the results you received:**

pull failed because signature can not be read/stored


**Describe the results you expected:**

pull succeeds


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

100%

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 15:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: I believe this is fixed in the main branch, we had a similar bug.  Could you verify that main has the fix?
We should have podman 3.0 rc1 available this week, if you can not build from the branch.

> open /home/ensc/.local/share/containers/sigstore

@QiWang19 @mtrmac, I am under the impression the hard-coded default in c/image is responsible.  Should we tweak it a bit to default to using `$GRAPHROOT/sigstore`?
Yes this should be able to be set via the API, it should not be hard coded.

sigstore has nothing to do with the local c/storage – it stages (or perhaps stores) signatures that conceptually belong into `docker://` registries.

There’s no reason to expect that clients like `skopeo sync` that may want to use sigstores have any c/storage store or configuration at all, so tying the default sigstore location to the c/storage graph root does not make any sense to me.

---

Specifically
> `chmod 000 ~/.local/share/containers`

IMHO fails quite clearly into the “don’t do that, then” category. There’s only so much that is reasonable to accommodate for unusual setups like $HOME not being used to actually store per-user data, or multiple separate per-user stores in a single account.

Sure, this is a quick and obvious way to reach those code paths, and the actual setup is probably less unusual.

---

The per-user directory sigstore can currently be redirected by creating a `registries.d/$foo.yaml` default configuration that points to a different `default-docker` sigstore; `registries.d` can be configured by a `types.SystemContext` passed to c/image (so, probably, a CLI option).

---

It’s a perfectly fair comment that there are too many separate configuration mechanisms and too many different ways paths are configured and defaulted; see also https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/849 .
Issue was explained and a work around was suggested, closing.
